svg.aria-label: Vanilla Claude Code vs. SHINE comparison
svg.title: Vanilla Claude Code vs. SHINE — side-by-side
svg.desc: What happens to the same prompt under a vanilla Claude Code install vs. a SHINE-enabled install, for four representative agency prompts.
svg.text: Vanilla Claude Code vs. SHINE — what changes, prompt by prompt
Same model. Same terminal. Different orchestration layer. Four representative prompts an agency actually types.
VANILLA CLAUDE CODE
WITH SHINE
PROMPT 1 · "Write a proposal for ACME's tech SEO project, MoSCoW, 40 MDs, 15% discount."
• Asks what "MoSCoW" and "MD" mean
• No memory of ACME — asks for the brief again
• Generic proposal template (English, no client tone)
• No Sources footer; invented KPIs possible
• Time-to-first-draft: ~10 minutes of back-and-forth
✓ Rule #19 fires — loads client-acme.md + style-proposal.md
✓ MoSCoW + MD + 15% baked in; Italian tone auto-applied
✓ 40 MDs × 8h mapped to phase plan automatically
✓ Verified-source watermark on any numbers
✓ First draft in < 60s — ready to review, not explain
PROMPT 2 · "Draft a follow-up to Jamie at CONTOSO about the workshop slot."
• Who's Jamie? What workshop? Which thread?
• Generic English, no CC, no subject convention
• No knowledge of tone history for CONTOSO
• Requires manually pasting past thread
• 6–8 revisions to match Kevin's voice
✓ shine-client-detect pre-loads client-contoso.md
✓ Gmail MCP pulls the last 3 thread messages automatically
✓ Rule #17: "CONTOSO | Workshop slot" subject, IT tone, CC rules
✓ style-contoso.md applied (past corrections baked in)
✓ 1–2 revisions max; often ready as-is
PROMPT 3 · "This cron job silently fails on Mondays. Why?"
• Starts from scratch; asks for logs
• May hallucinate the framework or library
• No guard against writing to .env or secrets
• No structured debug report — free-form reply
• You manually copy the fix into git
✓ Rule #3 → shine-debugger agent (hypothesis-driven)
✓ Rule #11 → context7 for accurate lib docs (no hallucination)
✓ shine-prompt-guard blocks any .env / key-shaped write (exit 2)
✓ 5-section debug report (symptom · hypothesis · test · fix · verify)
✓ Atomic commit with Conventional-Commits message suggested
PROMPT 4 · "Enrich this list of 40 Italian MarTech companies with contact emails."
• Guesses email patterns, no "inferred" labelling
• No verified-source discipline; may invent data
✓ Rule #20 → shine-lead-enrich · Apollo + Hunter + local script
✓ Watermark: verified / inferred / drafted labels on every row
